forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,32 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: b080
+title: 'serve: create .reviewignore at server start instead of waiting for the first review'
+---
+## What
+
+**Context.** `.reviewignore` is currently created lazily: `resolve_scope_files` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:613`) calls `ensure_reviewignore` on the first review of a repo. That means the file doesn't exist to edit until a review has already run once. Requested: create it at **server start**, so it's on disk (and editable ‚Äî e.g. to exclude upstream files in a fork) the moment `sah serve` comes up in a workspace.
+
+**Changes** (all in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs`):
+
+- [ ] Add a small helper `fn ensure_workspace_review_files(workspace_root: &std::path::Path)` that calls `swissarmyhammer_validators::review::ignore::ensure_reviewignore(workspace_root)` and downgrades any `Err` to a `tracing::warn!` ‚Äî server start must never fail over an unwritable ignore file.
+- [ ] Call it from `do_initialize_code_context` (~line 361) immediately after `resolve_workspace_root(work_dir)` succeeds and before the leadership/LSP work ‚Äî every `sah serve` (leader or follower) runs it. The existing `std::sync::Once` in `initialize_code_context` (~line 355) already makes this once-per-process; `ensure_reviewignore` itself is idempotent and never overwrites, so concurrent sibling servers racing at startup at worst both write the identical default template. A non-git/non-workspace cwd resolves no root and creates nothing, unchanged.
+- [ ] Keep the existing lazy `ensure_reviewignore` call in `resolve_scope_files` as the backstop for any review entry point that doesn't pass through this server init.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Starting the MCP server in a workspace with no `.reviewignore` leaves `<workspace_root>/.reviewignore` on disk containing the default template (header comment + `.kanban/`), before any review op has run.
+- [ ] A pre-existing `.reviewignore` is byte-identical after server start (user edits are never clobbered).
+- [ ] A failure to write the file (e.g. read-only root) logs a warning and server startup proceeds normally.
+- [ ] First-review creation still works when the file is absent (backstop path unchanged).
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Unit tests for `ensure_workspace_review_files` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` `#[cfg(test)]`: (a) temp dir without the file ‚Üí file created with the default template content; (b) temp dir with a custom `.reviewignore` ‚Üí content unchanged after the call; (c) unwritable dir ‚Üí returns without panicking.
+- [ ] Existing `ensure_reviewignore` tests in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/ignore.rs` (~lines 142/164) continue to pass, covering the backstop.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-tools` and `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators ignore` ‚Äî green; new tests are milliseconds (temp dirs only, no server spawn).
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

+++ modified

A non-git/non-workspace cwd resolves no root and creates nothing, unchanged.

-- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-tools` and `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators ignore` ‚Äî green; new tests are milliseconds (temp dirs only, no server spawn).
-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

-
-**Context.** `.reviewignore` is currently created lazily: `resolve_scope_files` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:613`) calls `ensure_reviewignore` on the first review of a repo. Requested: create it at **server start**, so it's on disk (and editable ‚Äî e.g. to exclude upstream files in a fork) the moment `sah serve` comes up in a workspace.
-
-**Changes** (all in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs`):
-
-- [ ] Add a small helper `fn ensure_workspace_review_files(workspace_root: &std::path::Path)` that calls `swissarmyhammer_validators::review::ignore::ensure_reviewignore(workspace_root)` and downgrades any `Err` to a `tracing::warn!` ‚Äî server start must never fail over an unwritable ignore file.
-- [ ] Call it from `do_initialize_code_context` (~line 361) immediately after `resolve_workspace_root(work_dir)` succeeds and before the leadership/LSP work ‚Äî every `sah serve` (leader or follower) runs it. A non-git/non-workspace cwd resolves no root and creates nothing, unchanged.
-- [ ] Keep the existing lazy `ensure_reviewignore` call in `resolve_scope_files` as the backstop for any review entry point that doesn't pass through this server init.

-- [ ] First-review creation still works when the file is absent (backstop path unchanged).

-- [ ] Unit tests for `ensure_workspace_review_files` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` `#[cfg(test)]`: (a) temp dir without the file ‚Üí file created with the default template content; (b) temp dir with a custom `.reviewignore` ‚Üí content unchanged after the call; (c) unwritable dir ‚Üí returns without panicking.

-

-- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

+## What\n\n**Context.** `.reviewignore` is currently created lazily: `resolve_scope_files` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:613`) calls `ensure_reviewignore` on the first review of a repo. That means the file doesn't exist to edit until a review has already run once. Requested: create it at **server start**, so it's on disk (and editable ‚Äî e.g. to exclude upstream files in a fork) the moment `sah serve` comes up in a workspace.\n\n**Changes** (all in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs`):\n\n- [ ] Add a small helper `fn ensure_workspace_review_files(workspace_root: &std::path::Path)` that calls `swissarmyhammer_validators::review::ignore::ensure_reviewignore(workspace_root)` and downgrades any `Err` to a `tracing::warn!` ‚Äî server start must never fail over an unwritable ignore file.\n- [ ] Call it from `do_initialize_code_context` (~line 361) immediately after `resolve_workspace_root(work_dir)` succeeds and before the leadership/LSP work ‚Äî every `sah serve` (leader or follower) runs it. The existing `std::sync::Once` in `initialize_code_context` (~line 355) already makes this once-per-process; `ensure_reviewignore` itself is idempotent and never overwrites, so concurrent sibling servers racing at startup at worst both write the identical default template. A non-git/non-workspace cwd resolves no root and creates nothing, unchanged.\n- [ ] Keep the existing lazy `ensure_reviewignore` call in `resolve_scope_files` as the backstop for any review entry point that doesn't pass through this server init.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n\n- [ ] Starting the MCP server in a workspace with no `.reviewignore` leaves `<workspace_root>/.reviewignore` on disk containing the default template (header comment + `.kanban/`), before any review op has run.\n- [ ] A pre-existing `.reviewignore` is byte-identical after server start (user edits are never clobbered).\n- [ ] A failure to write the file (e.g. read-only root) logs a warning and server startup proceeds normally.\n- [ ] First-review creation still works when the file is absent (backstop path unchanged).\n\n## Tests\n\n- [ ] Unit tests for `ensure_workspace_review_files` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` `#[cfg(test)]`: (a) temp dir without the file ‚Üí file created with the default template content; (b) temp dir with a custom `.reviewignore` ‚Üí content unchanged after the call; (c) unwritable dir ‚Üí returns without panicking.\n- [ ] Existing `ensure_reviewignore` tests in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/ignore.rs` (~lines 142/164) continue to pass, covering the backstop.\n- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-tools` and `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators ignore` ‚Äî green; new tests are milliseconds (temp dirs only, no server spawn).\n\n## Workflow\n- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.\n\n## Review Findings (2026-07-21 13:05)\n\nRan `review sha HEAD~1..HEAD` (commit cf8cc3de4) scoped to this checkpoint. The engine returned 11 findings, all confirmed by its own pipeline. Verified each against the actual file (both `HEAD` and `HEAD~1`, via direct read + grep) before acting, per prior incident notes that this engine has fabricated citations:\n\n- The commit's only changed hunks are `do_initialize_code_context` (adds the `ensure_workspace_review_files` call, current lines ~361-376), the new `ensure_workspace_review_files` fn (~440-463), and the three new `#[cfg(test)]` cases (~2574+). None of the 11 findings' cited line numbers fall inside these ranges.\n- Spot-checking the cited lines against file content: line 159/193 (cited for `McpServer` Debug derive / `pub tool_context`) are inside unrelated functions (`log_retry_attempt` / `retry_with_backoff`) in both `HEAD` and `HEAD~1` ‚Äî not the struct at all (real location: line 75/88). Lines 411/415 are cited for `format!("invalid model override...")` / `format!("failed to load configuration...")` strings that do not exist anywhere in the file (grep confirms zero matches) ‚Äî fabricated. `get_tool_registry` is cited at line 475 but the real definition is at line 1275. The `\"workspace mutex poisoned\"` and `\"<stdio>\"` sentinel findings describe real, pre-existing strings, but at 8 and 3 occurrences respectively (not the cited 5/2), all outside the diff.\n- Conclusion: none of the 11 findings pertain to this checkpoint's actual change (the new `ensure_workspace_review_files` helper, its call site, or its tests) ‚Äî they are pre-existing whole-file conditions the engine swept in, several with fabricated line/content citations. Nothing here is actionable against this diff.\n\nNo findings apply to the reviewed delta. Acceptance criteria and tests were already green (1766/1766, clippy/fmt clean) prior to this pass.
\ No newline at end of file

The existing `std::sync::Once` in `initialize_code_context` (~line 355) already makes this once-per-process; `ensure_reviewignore` itself is idempotent and never overwrites, so concurrent sibling servers racing at startup at worst both write the identical default template. A non-git/non-workspace cwd resolves no root and creates nothing, unchanged.
+- [ ] Keep the existing lazy `ensure_reviewignore` call in `resolve_scope_files` as the backstop for any review entry point that doesn't pass through this server init.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Starting the MCP server in a workspace with no `.reviewignore` leaves `<workspace_root>/.reviewignore` on disk containing the default template (header comment + `.kanban/`), before any review op has run.
+- [ ] A pre-existing `.reviewignore` is byte-identical after server start (user edits are never clobbered).
+- [ ] A failure to write the file (e.g. read-only root) logs a warning and server startup proceeds normally.
+- [ ] First-review creation still works when the file is absent (backstop path unchanged).
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Unit tests for `ensure_workspace_review_files` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` `#[cfg(test)]`: (a) temp dir without the file ‚Üí file created with the default template content; (b) temp dir with a custom `.reviewignore` ‚Üí content unchanged after the call; (c) unwritable dir ‚Üí returns without panicking.
+- [ ] Existing `ensure_reviewignore` tests in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/ignore.rs` (~lines 142/164) continue to pass, covering the backstop.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-tools` and `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators ignore` ‚Äî green; new tests are milliseconds (temp dirs only, no server spawn).

 title: 'serve: create .reviewignore at server start instead of waiting for the first review'
 ---
 ## What

**Context.** `.reviewignore` is currently created lazily: `resolve_scope_files` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:613`) calls `ensure_reviewignore` on the first review of a repo. That means the file doesn't exist to edit until a review has already run once. Requested: create it at **server start**, so it's on disk (and editable ‚Äî e.g. to exclude upstream files in a fork) the moment `sah serve` comes up in a workspace.

**Changes** (all in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs`):

- [ ] Add a small helper `fn ensure_workspace_review_files(workspace_root: &std::path::Path)` that calls `swissarmyhammer_validators::review::ignore::ensure_reviewignore(workspace_root)` and downgrades any `Err` to a `tracing::warn!` ‚Äî server start must never fail over an unwritable ignore file.
- [ ] Call it from `do_initialize_code_context` (~line 361) immediately after `resolve_workspace_root(work_dir)` succeeds and before the leadership/LSP work ‚Äî every `sah serve` (leader or follower) runs it. The existing `std::sync::Once` in `initialize_code_context` (~line 355) already makes this once-per-process; `ensure_reviewignore` itself is idempotent and never overwrites, so concurrent sibling servers racing at startup at worst both write the identical default template. A non-git/non-workspace cwd resolves no root and creates nothing, unchanged.
- [ ] Keep the existing lazy `ensure_reviewignore` call in `resolve_scope_files` as the backstop for any review entry point that doesn't pass through this server init.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Starting the MCP server in a workspace with no `.reviewignore` leaves `<workspace_root>/.reviewignore` on disk containing the default template (header comment + `.kanban/`), before any review op has run.
- [ ] A pre-existing `.reviewignore` is byte-identical after server start (user edits are never clobbered).
- [ ] A failure to write the file (e.g. read-only root) logs a warning and server startup proceeds normally.
- [ ] First-review creation still works when the file is absent (backstop path unchanged).

## Tests

- [ ] Unit tests for `ensure_workspace_review_files` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` `#[cfg(test)]`: (a) temp dir without the file ‚Üí file created with the default template content; (b) temp dir with a custom `.reviewignore` ‚Üí content unchanged after the call; (c) unwritable dir ‚Üí returns without panicking.
- [ ] Existing `ensure_reviewignore` tests in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/ignore.rs` (~lines 142/164) continue to pass, covering the backstop.
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-tools` and `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators ignore` ‚Äî green; new tests are milliseconds (temp dirs only, no server spawn).

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

## Review Findings (2026-07-21 13:05)

Ran `review sha HEAD~1..HEAD` (commit cf8cc3de4) scoped to this checkpoint. The engine returned 11 findings, all confirmed by its own pipeline. Verified each against the actual file (both `HEAD` and `HEAD~1`, via direct read + grep) before acting, per prior incident notes that this engine has fabricated citations:

- The commit's only changed hunks are `do_initialize_code_context` (adds the `ensure_workspace_review_files` call, current lines ~361-376), the new `ensure_workspace_review_files` fn (~440-463), and the three new `#[cfg(test)]` cases (~2574+). None of the 11 findings' cited line numbers fall inside these ranges.
- Spot-checking the cited lines against file content: line 159/193 (cited for `McpServer` Debug derive / `pub tool_context`) are inside unrelated functions (`log_retry_attempt` / `retry_with_backoff`) in both `HEAD` and `HEAD~1` ‚Äî not the struct at all (real location: line 75/88). Lines 411/415 are cited for `format!("invalid model override...")` / `format!("failed to load configuration...")` strings that do not exist anywhere in the file (grep confirms zero matches) ‚Äî fabricated. `get_tool_registry` is cited at line 475 but the real definition is at line 1275. The `"workspace mutex poisoned"` and `"<stdio>"` sentinel findings describe real, pre-existing strings, but at 8 and 3 occurrences respectively (not the cited 5/2), all outside the diff.
- Conclusion: none of the 11 findings pertain to this checkpoint's actual change (the new `ensure_workspace_review_files` helper, its call site, or its tests) ‚Äî they are pre-existing whole-file conditions the engine swept in, several with fabricated line/content citations. Nothing here is actionable against this diff.

No findings apply to the reviewed delta. Acceptance criteria and tests were already green (1766/1766, clippy/fmt clean) prior to this pass.
